meta.title: BMW S62 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: “The S62 V8 is a legend – but rod bearing and VANOS wear are known issues. Get honest UK specialist quotes and maintenance advice today.”

@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the S62

mainEntity.name: How much does an S62 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned S62 typically costs £5,000–£8,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by exact condition and warranty. Fitting adds £900–£1,700 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW S62 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the S62 at 4.0 stars out of 5, based on a 90% confidence level from specialist reports and UK owner surveys [ESTIMATED]. The engine is robust, but VANOS and rod bearings require proactive attention – they are not reliability faults but maintenance items.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common S62 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rod bearing wear is the most serious – often appearing around 80,000–100,000 miles, especially on cars that have seen hard use. VANOS solenoid and seal failures are also common, causing low‑end hesitation and loss of top‑end power.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the S62?

acceptedAnswer.text: The S62 was fitted exclusively to the E39 M5 (1998–2003) and the E52 Z8 (1999–2003). Both are low‑volume, high‑performance BMWs that are now collectable.

mainEntity.name: How long does the S62 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance – regular oil changes, VANOS rebuilds at 80,000 miles, and replacing rod bearings as a preventative measure – the S62 can easily exceed 150,000 miles. Many well‑maintained examples are still on the road today.

hero.h1: BMW S62 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW S62 is a 4.9‑litre naturally aspirated V8 – the heart of the E39 M5, revered for its character but with known VANOS and rod bearing wear. Backed by UK specialist data [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% – based on cross‑referenced specialist reports and UK owner surveys.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of straightforward sensor/coil replacements and labour‑intensive VANOS/rod bearing work)
12 (Good OEM and aftermarket support, but some VANOS components are becoming harder to source)
8 (Recon cost ~65% of lowest typical E39 M5 value (£10,000) → 8 (60–74% bracket))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The S62 is a charismatic V8 with immense character – but rod bearing and VANOS wear are common beyond 80,000 miles – buy only with documented servicing.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts who want a classic M car experience and are prepared for maintenance costs.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You want a low‑maintenance daily driver or aren't willing to invest in preventative work.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW – no cross‑brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an S62 typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
